risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Participants will be randomly divided into two groups (A and B) of equal size using a computer-generated randomisation schedule with permuted blocks of random size prepared by the study statistician. The randomisation schedule will not be stratified..." Comment: This explicitly describes a proper random sequence generation method (computer-generated randomization with permuted blocks of random size), which is sufficient for Low risk.

Quote: "The study medication (nortriptyline or identical placebo) will be packaged in identical containers. Each container will be pre-labelled (by the study pharmacist contracted to provide the study medication) with a study identifier according to randomisation schedule... Study numbers and study medication will be issued sequentially to participants." Comment: This describes a pharmacist-controlled, sequentially issued medication system with identical containers and pre-labelling according to the randomisation schedule, which would prevent the recruiter/research nurse from foreseeing the next assignment before enrollment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The study investigators, the research nurse dispensing the medication and assessing outcomes, and the participants will remain blind to the treatment allocation." Comment: This explicitly states that both participants and key study personnel were blinded, and the trial used "identical placebo capsules" in identical containers. Because the main outcomes (pain, function, global assessment) are subjective and could be influenced by knowledge of allocation, successful blinding supports a low risk judgement for performance bias.

Quote: "The study investigators, the research nurse dispensing the medication and assessing outcomes, and the participants will remain blind to the treatment allocation." Comment: Outcome assessment was performed by the research nurse, who was explicitly blinded to allocation. The main outcomes (WOMAC pain/function, participant global assessment, SF36) are structured questionnaire/participant-rated measures, and because both the assessor and participant were blinded, knowledge of allocation was unlikely to influence measurement.

Quote: "Data analysis will be performed on an intention-to-treat basis" and "Participants who are missing outcome data will be included in the analysis using modern multiple imputation methods." Comment: The trial reports ITT analysis with an appropriate missing-data approach. In addition, the review record shows all randomized participants were included in the analysis (placebo 103/103; nortriptyline 102/102), indicating essentially complete outcome data and no evidence that attrition could bias the results.
